channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to watch Mayor of Kingstown season 4 online from anywhere – stream Taylor Sheridan crime drama
item.description: Jeremy Renner stars as the "Mayor" looking to stop all out gang war as the hit show returns. Here's how to watch Mayor of Kingstown season 4 online no matter where in the world you are.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wardley, TechRadar

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Personas y tecnología, un binomio ganador en ING
item.description: Solo es posible alcanzar el éxito en la transformación digital empresarial si ésta va acompañada de una sólida cultura basada en las personas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI: Latest news and insights
item.description: OpenAI is an artificial intelligence organization comprised of the non-profit OpenAI, Inc. and several for-profit subsidiaries. The company is perhaps best known for its ChatGPT chatbot, which launched in 2022, kicking off a period of massive disruption in the tech industry and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Patmos set up an AI data center in 90 days
item.description: Data center hosting and compute services provider Patmos set up and deployed a new AI data center in just 90 days, when it usually takes two to three years to set up a colocation facility. All it needed was an unused printing press in the heart of Kansas City.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rare dinosaur mummies help scientists recreate their prehistoric lives
item.description: Researchers have unearthed a spooky pair of dinosaur mummies that seem to have been preserved in an unexpected way.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dinosaurs were thriving in North America before the mass-extinction asteroid strike, study suggests
item.description: New research suggests that dinosaur populations were thriving in North America before the asteroid impact 66 million years ago that caused mass extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Celtic vs Sturm Graz live stream – How to watch Europa League online, FREE trial, kick-off time
item.description: Celtic take on Sturm Graz: European night sparks, Austrian counter-press chaos, and Kyogo Furuhashi magic — who will roar the loudest in the Europa League 2025?
item.dc:creator: Tom Bailey, TechRadar

item.title: The hidden data problem killing enterprise AI projects
item.description: Headlines alternate between massive AI investments and reports of failed deployments. The pattern is consistent across industries: seemingly promising AI projects that work well in testing environments struggle or fail when deployed in real-world conditions.
item.dc:creator: Brian Moore, Fast Company

item.title: Who sets the rules for AI? It matters more than most people realize
item.description: Governing AI is "a matter of incredible economic importance of national security significance," said Kevin Frazier. The post Who sets the rules for AI? It matters more than most people realize first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Delete the fake VPN app stealing Android users' money
item.description: Cleafy researchers discover fake VPN streaming app Mobdro Pro that installs Klopatra banking Trojan, giving attackers full control over Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Extreme brings AI agent to Platform ONE for swift network diagnostics, troubleshooting
item.description: Extreme Networks has officially worked its Service AI Agent into its flagship network management package, Platform ONE, to give customers rapid access to diagnostics, autonomous troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, dashboards, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Using the SkiaSharp graphics library in .NET
item.description: The news that the .NET UI framework Uno Platform project would be upstreaming features and fixes into the core multiplatform .NET libraries makes a lot of sense. The open source project has been working on cross-device .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Prince Harry, Meghan join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, are joining a call to halt the development of some AI tools until the process can be "done safely and controllably.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Riverbed tackles AI data bottleneck with new Oracle-based service
item.description: Riverbed recently unveiled Data Express Service, a SaaS offering the company developed in partnership with Oracle that is designed to address the challenge of securely moving massive datasets quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World
